title: Attachments
drDigitalIntro: You can attach files to emails — photos, documents, PDFs, anything. Click the paperclip icon and choose a file from your computer. The file gets sent with your email. The recipient can open or download the file. Large files take longer to send. Some email services have limits on attachment size — if your file is too big, send it with a file-sharing service instead.

playgroundTask.question: How do you add a file to an email before sending it?

options.text: Paste the file's name in the message body
Click the paperclip icon and choose a file
Print the file first, then scan it

drDigitalSuccess: The paperclip icon is the universal symbol for attachments — click it to add any file from your computer.
drDigitalHint: Look for a paperclip icon in the email composer toolbar.
